claim_boundary: NEST organism per-neuron state dimensionality diagnostic only. Not mechanism promotion, not a baseline freeze, not public usefulness proof, not hardware/native transfer, and not a claim about the standalone or C runtime performance.

controls.description: Per-polyp activity_rate PR — the current measurement method.
controls.name: current_per_polyp_aggregate

controls.description: Per-neuron spikes with per_polyp_input_diversity=False. Tests whether input diversity is necessary for per-neuron PR gain.
controls.name: no_input_diversity

hypothesis: Reading 512 per-neuron spike channels (16 polyps x 32 neurons) captures state diversity that per-polyp aggregation collapses. With per-polyp input diversity enabled, different neurons within each polyp respond to different input gains, creating independent spike patterns that achieve PR comparable to standalone.

measurement.pr_computation: Standard PR on test split covariance via geometry_metrics from tier7_7j
measurement.state_vector: Per-step per-neuron spike counts (total spikes per neuron per sim.run() window)

null_hypothesis: Per-neuron spike channels are highly correlated because all neurons within a polyp receive similar input drive regardless of input gain diversity. PR stays below 2.0 even with per-neuron readout, confirming the gap to standalone is fundamental.

outcome_classes.action: Document that spiking state dimensionality matches standalone when measured at per-neuron resolution.
outcome_classes.condition: primary pass AND sham separated AND ablation confirms

outcome_classes.action: Document partial signal with remaining gap explained.
outcome_classes.condition: PR improves but does not reach 4.0 or 2x ratio

outcome_classes.action: The gap to standalone is fundamental to spiking LIF architecture at current configuration.
outcome_classes.condition: No meaningful PR improvement over per-polyp aggregate

outcome_classes.action: PR gain is from raw channel count, not organized structure.
outcome_classes.condition: Shuffled assignment matches or exceeds candidate

pass_criteria.description: 16-polyp per-neuron PR > 4.0 AND per-neuron PR > 2x per-polyp aggregate PR
pass_criteria.name: primary_pass

pass_criteria.description: Shuffled neuron assignment PR < candidate PR - 2.0
pass_criteria.name: sham_separation

question: Does the NEST organism's per-neuron spike state measured via get_per_neuron_spike_vector() achieve state dimensionality (PR > 4.0) comparable to the standalone edge-of-chaos reference (PR=7.0), and exceed 2x the per-polyp aggregate PR, or is the gap fundamental to spiking LIF architecture?

shams.description: Same per-step neuron spike counts, but each neuron randomly reassigned to a different polyp. Tests whether organized input diversity structure is causal or any 512 random spike channels give the same PR.
shams.name: shuffled_neuron_assignment
shams.passes_if: Shuffled assignment PR < candidate PR - 2.0, confirming polyp structure matters.

shams.description: Same neuron spike counts, same polyp assignment, but spike times shuffled within each neuron's spike train. Tests whether temporal spike structure matters or only total spike counts.
shams.name: permuted_spike_temporal
shams.passes_if: Temporal permutation PR < candidate PR, confirming temporal structure matters.
